changes.motivation: Eliminate a manual step where users had to download the SAML metadata XML from the Authentik Web UI during SSO setup
changes.summary: Updated `setupFederatedAuth.ts` to programmatically retrieve the SAML metadata using `terragruntOutput()` on the `saml_metadata` output from the deployed `authentik_aws_sso` Terraform module, then save it to `[clusterPath]/authentik_aws_sso/saml_metadata.xml` via `writeFile()`. The AWS IAM Identity Center upload prompt now shows the saved file path instead of asking the user to manually download from Authentik.

problems: The session spent significant time attempting to fix pre-existing, unrelated website lint and typecheck failures (missing `globals` package, `eslint-plugin-solid`/`@typescript-eslint` version mismatch with eslint 10, `bunx eslint` parser resolution failure when run from repo root). These were all pre-existing on `main` but only surfaced because the pre-commit hook runs on changed files and the FOOTER.md doc change triggered the website typecheck. All these changes were ultimately reverted. The root cause was undocumented: the website ESLint setup is broken on this branch (eslint 10 incompatible with `eslint-plugin-solid`, `astro-eslint-parser` not hoisted to root), and the pre-commit hooks have no documented way to skip unrelated failing checks.

The agent created and then had to remove documentation changes (FOOTER.md for `authentik_aws_sso`, website federated-auth guide update) that were out of scope for what the user wanted. The PRD planning phase should have clarified earlier that doc changes were not desired, rather than implementing them and reverting.

The `reference.mdx` file in the infrastructure module is auto-generated by a commit hook from `FOOTER.md`. This relationship is not documented in either file, causing confusion when the hook modified `reference.mdx` unexpectedly after a `FOOTER.md` edit.
